title: SOURCE NEEMISHA BANSAL 120 GRAMMAR RULES TOPIC ERROR QU

questions.question: [RULE 91: "ADAPT / ADJUST / ACCUSTOMED" + TO + V1-ING]
options: (a) He is (b) accustomed (c) to (d) sleep late at night.
questions.explanation: 'Accustomed to', 'Look forward to', 'With a view to' ke baad hamesha gerund (V1+ing) aata hai. 'Sleeping' sahi hai.

questions.question: Q902. (a) I am (b) looking forward (c) to (d) meet you.
questions.explanation: 'Looking forward to' ke baad 'meeting' (gerund) aayega. Ye ek fixed phrase hai.

questions.question: Q903. (a) He (b) adapted (c) himself (d) to the new environment.
questions.explanation: 'Adapt' ke saath reflexive pronoun aur 'to' ka use bilkul sahi hai.

questions.question: Q904. (a) She (b) is (c) used to (d) drive on the left.
questions.explanation: Rule 55 ki tarah, 'Be + used to' ke baad gerund 'driving' aana chahiye.

questions.question: Q905. (a) With a (b) view to (c) help the poor, (d) he started an NGO.
questions.explanation: 'With a view to' ke baad hamesha V1+ing lagta hai. 'Helping' sahi hoga.

questions.question: [RULE 92: "NOBODY ELSE" + BUT]
options: (a) It is (b) nobody else (c) than (d) my brother.
questions.explanation: 'Else' ke saath hamesha 'but' ka pair banta hai, 'than' ka nahi. 'Nobody else but' sahi hai.

questions.question: Q907. (a) He (b) wants (c) nothing else (d) but peace.
questions.explanation: 'Nothing else but' ka prayog bilkul correct hai.

questions.question: Q908. (a) I (b) have (c) no one else (d) than you to help me.
questions.explanation: 'No one else but you' hona chahiye.

questions.question: Q909. (a) It (b) was (c) nothing else (d) than pride.
questions.explanation: 'Than' ko 'but' se badlein.

questions.question: Q910. (a) Who (b) else (c) but (d) him can do this?
questions.explanation: 'Else...but' ka use yahan sahi hai.

questions.question: [RULE 93: "PROHIBIT / PREVENT / ABSTAIN" + FROM + V1-ING]
options: (a) He (b) prohibited (c) me (d) to enter the room.
questions.explanation: 'Prohibit' ke baad 'from + gerund' ka use hota hai. 'From entering' sahi hai.

questions.question: Q912. (a) You (b) should (c) abstain (d) to drink.
questions.explanation: 'Abstain' (door rehna) ke saath 'from + drinking' aayega.

questions.question: Q913. (a) He (b) prevented (c) me (d) from going there.
questions.explanation: 'Prevent from + V1-ing' bilkul sahi structure hai.

questions.question: Q914. (a) She (b) was (c) debarred (d) to give the exam.
questions.explanation: 'Debar' ke saath 'from giving' ka use karein.

questions.question: Q915. (a) I (b) desist (c) from (d) No Error.
questions.explanation: 'Desist' ke saath 'from' ka prayog sahi hai.

questions.question: [RULE 94: "THE" WITH SUPERLATIVE DEGREE]
options: (a) He is (b) (c) best (d) student in the class.
questions.explanation: Superlative degree (best, tallest, etc.) ke pehle hamesha 'The' lagta hai.

questions.question: Q917. (a) This is (b) the (c) most (d) difficult task.
questions.explanation: 'The most difficult' sahi superlative form hai.

questions.question: Q918. (a) She is (b) (c) tallest (d) of all the girls.
questions.explanation: 'The tallest' hona chahiye.

questions.question: Q919. (a) Honesty (b) is (c) the (d) best policy.
questions.explanation: Famous proverb mein 'the best' sahi hai.

questions.question: Q920. (a) It (b) was (c) (d) worst day of my life.
questions.explanation: 'The worst' ka use karein kyunki ye superlative degree hai.

questions.question: [RULE 95: "SUFFICIENT / ENOUGH" (REDUNDANCY)]
options: (a) This (b) amount (c) is (d) quite enough for me.
questions.explanation: 'Quite' aur 'Enough' ka matlab lagbhag ek hi hota hai. Dono ko saath use karna galat hai. Sirf 'enough' ya 'quite sufficient' kahein.

questions.question: Q922. (a) He (b) has (c) enough (d) money.
questions.explanation: 'Enough' noun se pehle aaya hai, jo sahi hai.

questions.question: Q923. (a) The (b) food (c) is (d) more than enough.
questions.explanation: 'More than enough' ek sahi phrase hai.

questions.question: Q924. (a) It (b) is (c) sufficient (d) enough.
questions.explanation: 'Sufficient' aur 'Enough' ka saath mein use redundancy hai. Ek ko hata dein.

questions.question: Q925. (a) We (b) have (c) quite (d) sufficient resources.
questions.explanation: 'Quite sufficient' ka prayog sahi hai.

questions.question: [RULE 96: "BETWEEN" FOR MORE THAN TWO (MUTUAL RELATION)]
options: (a) There is (b) a tournament (c) between (d) the four nations.
questions.explanation: Jab mutual relationship ya alliance ho, toh 2 se zyada ke liye bhi 'between' sahi hai. No Error.

questions.question: Q927. (a) Divide (b) this (c) among (d) the two brothers.
questions.explanation: Do ke liye hamesha 'between' ka use karein.

questions.question: Q928. (a) There (b) is (c) cooperation (d) between all the countries.
questions.explanation: Mutual agreement/cooperation ke liye 'between' bilkul sahi hai chahe sankhya zyada ho.

questions.question: Q929. (a) Negotiation (b) between (c) the (d) three parties failed.
questions.explanation: Specific parties ke beech ke relation ke liye 'between' correct hai.

questions.question: Q930. (a) He (b) sat (c) between (d) the students.
questions.explanation: Yahan koi specific relationship nahi hai, sirf position hai, isliye 'among' hona chahiye.

questions.question: [RULE 97: "SENIOR / JUNIOR" (OBJECTIVE CASE AFTER 'TO')]
questions.explanation: Preposition 'to' ke baad hamesha objective case (me) aata hai, subjective (I) nahi.

questions.question: Q932. (a) She (b) is (c) junior (d) to him.
questions.explanation: 'Junior to him' (objective case) bilkul sahi hai.

questions.question: Q933. (a) They (b) are (c) superior (d) to us.
questions.explanation: 'To' ke baad 'us' objective case hai, jo sahi hai.

questions.question: Q934. (a) Ram (b) is (c) elder (d) than me.
questions.explanation: 'Elder' ke saath 'to' lagta hai. 'Elder to me' sahi hai.

questions.question: Q935. (a) Prior (b) to (c) she (d) leaving, we talked.
questions.explanation: 'Prior to' ke baad objective case ya possessive (her leaving) aayega.

questions.question: [RULE 98: "REASON IS" + THAT (NOT BECAUSE)]
options: (a) The (b) reason why (c) he failed (d) is because he didn't study.
questions.explanation: 'The reason' ya 'The reason why' ke saath kabhi 'because' nahi lagta kyunki dono ka matlab karan batana hai. 'Is that' ka use karein.

questions.question: Q937. (a) The (b) reason (c) is (d) that he was ill.
questions.explanation: 'The reason is that' bilkul sahi structure hai.

questions.question: Q938. (a) The (b) reason (c) why he (d) is late is because of the rain.
questions.explanation: 'Because of' ko 'that' se badlein.

questions.question: Q939. (a) Reason (b) for (c) his (d) absence is that he is busy.
questions.explanation: 'Is that' ka use sahi hai.

questions.question: Q940. (a) The (b) reason (c) is (d) due to his laziness.
questions.explanation: 'The reason' ke saath 'due to' ya 'because' nahi 'that' lagayein.

questions.question: [RULE 99: "HARDLY / SCARCELY" (INVERSION)]
options: (a) Hardly (b) I (c) had (d) stepped out when it rained.
questions.explanation: Sentence 'Hardly' se shuru ho toh inversion hoga: 'Hardly had I'.

questions.question: Q942. (a) Scarcely (b) had (c) the (d) teacher entered when the students stood up.
questions.explanation: Inversion (had + teacher) aur pair (when) dono sahi hain.

questions.question: Q943. (a) Hardly (b) did (c) he (d) went there.
questions.explanation: 'Did' ke saath V1 (go) aani chahiye.

questions.question: Q944. (a) Scarcely (b) he (c) reached (d) when he fell ill.
questions.explanation: Inversion karein: 'Scarcely had he reached'.

questions.question: Q945. (a) Hardly (b) had (c) he (d) seen the lion than he ran.
questions.explanation: 'Hardly' ke saath 'when' lagta hai, 'than' nahi.

questions.question: [RULE 100: "THE SAME...THAT" / "THE SAME...AS"]
options: (a) This (b) is (c) the same (d) book which I wanted.
questions.explanation: 'The same' ke baad relative pronoun 'that' ka use hota hai. 'Which' hata dein.

questions.question: Q947. (a) He (b) is (c) the same (d) boy as helped me.
questions.explanation: Agar aage verb (helped) di ho, toh 'that' ka use karein. 'The same boy that helped me'.

questions.question: Q948. (a) My (b) pen (c) is (d) the same as yours.
questions.explanation: Jab comparison dikhana ho aur aage verb na ho, toh 'the same as' sahi hai.

questions.question: Q949. (a) This (b) is (c) the same (d) man that I saw yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'The same...that' yahan bilkul sahi hai.

questions.question: Q950. (a) It (b) is (c) the same (d) which I gave you.
questions.explanation: 'Which' ki jagah 'that' lagayein.
